===Pokémon===
[[File:Goh Galar first partners.png|thumb|250px|left|Goh's current [[party]]]]
Because one of his goals is to {{pkmn2|caught|catch}} every {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} and complete his [[Pokédex]], Goh catches a lot of Pokémon species, including multiple members of the same [[Evolution|evolutionary line]] and Pokémon that have [[List of Pokémon with branched Evolutions|branched Evolutions]], as well as Pokémon that have [[form]] differences and a [[regional form]]. Currently, Goh is known to own 108 Pokémon, with 115 or 116<ref group="lower-alpha">The identity of Goh's last on-screen capture has not been revealed.</ref> unique Pokémon species registered in his Pokédex. During his journey, Goh has encountered some Trainers with similar goals, such as [[Shane Seeker]], who seeks to capture all [[Shiny Pokémon]], and [[Kricketina Kylie]], a {{type|Bug}} Pokémon collector.

In ''[[JN006|Working My Way Back to Mew!]]'', Goh managed to capture all [[Generation I]] Bug-type Pokémon, which, according to Kricketina Kylie, makes him Kanto's "Bug Pokéking". During his research, Goh has access to several of his Pokémon, rotating between them. As of ''[[JN136|This Could be the Start of Something Big!]]'', Goh decided to take a journey through the [[Kanto]] region, leaving all of his Pokémon (with the exception of the [[Galar first partner Pokémon]]) at the [[Cerise Laboratory]].

This listing is of Goh's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:

====Released====
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Goh
|pkmn=Floette
|stage1=Flabébé
|type1=Fairy
|gender=female
|img=Goh Flabébé White Flower.png
|cap=Goh's White Flower Flabébé
|img2=Goh Floette White Flower.png
|cap2=Goh's White Flower Floette
|epnum=JN025
|epname=A Festival Reunion!
|vajp=Marina Aicholtz
|vaen=Marina Aicholtz
|desc={{DL|List of Pokémon with form differences|Flabébé, Floette, and Florges|White Flower}} {{p|Floette}} was encountered in [[Shalour City]] as a {{p|Flabébé}}, but her capture was not confirmed until ''[[JN031|The Cuteness Quotient!]]''. In ''[[JN066|Under Color of Darkness!]]'', her flower was revealed to have lost a petal, causing her to be unable to fly properly on it anymore. Ash and Goh traveled to [[Kalos]] to find a new flower for her, eventually succeeding. Almost immediately afterwards, she evolved into Floette. Goh then decided to {{pkmn2|released|release}} her so that she could grow and protect a garden of white flowers for other Flabébé.

Floette's only known move is {{m|Grassy Terrain}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Goh
|pkmn=Ninetales
|type1=Ice
|type2=Fairy
|img=Goh Alolan Ninetales.png
|epnum=JN071
|epname=Leaping Toward the Dream!
|vajp=Risa Shimizu
|vaen=Emily Jenness
|desc=This {{rf|Alolan}} {{p|Ninetales}} is a Pokémon that was originally stolen from its home on [[Mount Lanakila]] by [[Pokémon hunter]]s. It managed to get away from them when their plane crashed, and wound up on [[Mount Coronet]] in [[Sinnoh]], as it reminded it of its home. Goh was tasked by [[Project Mew]] to catch it so that it could be returned to its home. Goh failed to catch it during his initial encounter with it, but during the second encounter, he managed to convince it to let itself be caught so that it could be taken back home.

In ''[[JN080|Trial on a Golden Scale!]]'', Project Mew members took Ninetales back to [[Alola]].

Ninetales's known moves are {{m|Ice Beam}}, {{m|Blizzard}}, {{m|Dazzling Gleam}}, and {{m|Aurora Veil}}.}}

Cyndaquil's only known move is {{m|Flamethrower}}.}}

|desc={{p|Pinsir}} was encountered in [[Vermilion Forest]]. [[Kricketina Kylie]] originally intended to {{pkmn2|caught|catch}} it for herself, but due to her being trapped in one of {{TRT}}'s nets, Goh was forced to catch it instead. He later [[trade]]d it for one of Kricketina's {{TP|Goh|Heracross}}.

None of Pinsir's moves are known.}}

====Given away====
{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Leon
|pkmn=Eternatus
|type1=Poison
|type2=Dragon
|img=Goh Eternatus.png
|img2=Eternatus Eternamax anime.png
|epnum=JN012
|epname=Flash of the Titans!
|vajp=
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Eternatus}} first appeared in ''[[JN012|Flash of the Titans!]]'', where it flew past a plane that Ash and Goh were riding to [[Galar]].

In ''[[JN044|Sword and Shield: "From Here to Eternatus!"]]'', Eternatus was revealed to have been captured by [[Macro Cosmos]] and placed inside the [[Energy Plant]] core. After it escaped, [[Rose]] urged {{an|Leon}} to catch it. In [[JN045|the next episode]], Leon's attempt to catch Eternatus failed, and it subsequently turned into its {{DL|List of Pokémon with form differences|Eternatus|Eternamax}} form. With the help of {{p|Zacian}} and {{p|Zamazenta}}, Ash and Goh defeated Eternatus, after which they threw one of Goh's {{i|Poké Ball}}s at it together, catching it. Eternatus's Poké Ball was subsequently sealed away by [[Professor Magnolia]].

In ''[[JN127|Just a Scone's Throw From Here!]]'', Eternatus was revealed to have been unsealed, with Professor Magnolia, [[Sonia]], and Leon hoping to achieve coexistence between it and the people of Galar. As such, Goh decided to entrust Eternatus to Leon, who accepted the offer.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Goh
|pkmn=Dugtrio
|type1=Ground
|img=Goh Dugtrio.png
|epnum=JN052
|epname=How Are You Gonna Keep 'Em Off of the Farm?
|vajp=
|vaen=Marc Thompson
|desc={{p|Dugtrio}} was the leader of a group of {{p|Diglett}} that were eating the crops at [[Laxton Farm]]. It was ultimately caught by Goh in order to stop them. However, after Goh realized Diglett and Dugtrio also help cultivate the soil, he gave Dugtrio to [[Laxton]] so that it could continue to work for the benefit of the farm.

Dugtrio's known moves are {{m|Dig}}, {{m|Bulldoze}}, and {{m|Sand Tomb}}.}}

====Status unknown====
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Goh
|pkmn=Kingdra
|type1=Water
|type2=Dragon
|img=Goh Kingdra.png
|epnum=JN088
|epname=Looking Out for Number Two!
|vajp=
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Kingdra}} was targeted by Goh for his second [[Project Mew]] [[Field Research|Trial Mission]]. After locating it in an underwater cave off the coast of [[Slateport City]], he battled against it and managed to catch it. When Ash and Goh's air supply started to run out, Goh had Kingdra propel them to the surface.

Kingdra has not been seen since its debut episode, making it unclear whether Goh kept it following the Trial Mission.

Kingdra's only known move is {{m|Dragon Breath}}.}}

====Temporary====

|pkmn=Pikachu

|cap=Goh with Ash's Pikachu

|desc=In ''[[JN026|Slowking's Crowning!]]'', Goh had {{p|Pikachu}} use {{m|Thunderbolt}} to try and force open a {{p|Shellder}} that a {{p|Slowking}} had placed on {{Ash}}'s head, causing him to act strangely. However, Ash was able to dodge the attack, and the Thunderbolt hit a group of {{p|Slowpoke}} instead.

In ''[[JN072|Everybody's Doing the Underground Shuffle!]]'', Goh and Pikachu wound up separated from Ash, {{TP|Goh|Grookey}}, [[Chloe]], and {{TP|Chloe|Eevee}} while running away from {{TRT}} in a {{p|Durant}} nest under [[Driftveil City]]. While looking for their friends, Goh used Pikachu to battle a {{p|Seismitoad}}, but Pikachu kept taking the initative before Goh could command him, preventing Goh from catching it. Goh and Pikachu later battled a Durant, but were forced to flee when a swarm overtook them.}}
